title: Campaign State
description: P-B. Persisted, resumable state for the Campaign runner (design §5.4a) — the campaign analogue of the Driver's state.json (§4.5). Holds the cursor over the milestone backlog, per-unit status + audit linkage, cumulative countable spend, and the pause reason when halted at a human-authority gate.

status.description: running = mid-flight; paused = halted at a human gate (see pause_reason); done = backlog exhausted + accepted; ended = aborted.

pause_reason.description: When paused: the driver/campaign checkpoint_id that caused the halt (the resume map key).

pause_checkpoint.description: When paused: path to the checkpoint file the human resolves.

followup_baseline_seq.description: Internal: the milestone's subsprint_sequence SNAPSHOT recorded at a deliver_followup_required pause, so resume advances ONLY to a NEWLY inserted follow-up (an id not in this snapshot, at cursor+1) — never to a pre-existing or appended-elsewhere sub-sprint.

milestone_context.description: The ACTIVE milestone's git isolation context (campaign-tier Loop Ingress). Persisted for resume; cleared when the milestone cursor advances past a merge gate.

work_dir.description: Directory sub-sprints run in (repo root or worktree path).

repo_dir.description: The main git admin dir (.orchestrator lives here).

pending_milestone_advance.description: Internal: the current milestone is DONE but the cursor has NOT advanced yet — waiting at milestone_merge.

freshness_block.description: Track-2 T2-A/B4 — the DURABLE freshness-block overlay. When a mid-run universal F1 freshness gate (_authority_fresh) detects that the signed plan went STALE (a post-sign authority/scope edit, or budget raise without re-sign), the campaign BLOCKS for re-sign as pause_reason='campaign_plan_signoff' WHILE preserving the ORIGINAL gate here, so a post-re-sign resume returns to that original gate (mechanism-A resume, deliver_followup_required, milestone_merge, decision-bound checkpoints). It is persisted campaign-state (NOT an in-memory flag) so a crash mid-block never loses the original gate. Additive; absent/null ⇒ no freshness block is active (byte-identical to today).

original_pause_reason.description: The pause_reason the campaign was at when the freshness gate fired — restored verbatim on a post-re-sign resume.

original_pause_checkpoint.description: The pause_checkpoint that accompanied original_pause_reason — restored alongside it so the original gate's decision file/nonce binding is unchanged.

engine_restamp.description: Track-2 T2-A/TD6 — the engine-authored deliver_followup re-stamp record. The ONE legitimate mid-campaign plan mutation (a follow-up sub-sprint inserted at cursor+1) grows subsprint_sequence, which is inside the single signed hash, so the live plan would read 'stale'. Rather than write back the signed plan (re-authorization requires an explicit Human signoff artifact), the engine advances the SINGLE signed epoch by pinning the authorized signed_scope_hash here plus append-only provenance. Each invocation deterministically RE-APPLIES it to the in-memory signoff iff the live hash still equals signed_scope_hash, so every freshness consumer agrees (no divergence) and any FURTHER plan edit (changing the live hash) is refused. Additive; absent/null ⇒ no re-stamp has occurred (byte-identical to today).

signed_scope_hash.description: The pinned authorized epoch hash (sha256 of the signed-scope object H over the live, grown plan at re-stamp time). The cross-invocation proof: the live plan is effectively signed iff its recomputed live hash equals this.

restamp_version.description: Monotonic count of engine re-stamps (one per authorized deliver_followup insertion).

deltas.description: Append-only provenance for every authorized deliver_followup insertion the engine advanced the epoch for (audit; the pinned signed_scope_hash is the integrity proof).

subsprint_id.description: The newly inserted follow-up sub-sprint id.

at_index.description: The cursor+1 index the follow-up was inserted at in the milestone's subsprint_sequence.

authorizing_checkpoint.description: The deliver_followup_required pause checkpoint that authorized the insertion.

prior_signed_scope_hash.description: The signed_scope_hash before this re-stamp (epoch chain).

gap_followup_state.description: Track 2 Phase 2-γ / Constitution §1.7-F clause 2-3. Persisted RUNTIME state for the pre-authorized in-envelope completeness-remediation auto-route — the basis of the runtime bounds the static charter validator cannot enforce. Additive; absent ⇒ no gap-followup has run (byte-identical to today). The §1.7-F engine (campaign:_gap_followup_round) is DORMANT unless a requirement ledger is wired AND the plan is fresh-signed AND the post-close gap_report (scope_report.build_gap_report, source-sealed) is non-empty.

rounds_by_milestone.description: Per-milestone remediation-round counter (§1.7-F clause 2: gap_followup.max_subsprints per milestone). Keyed by milestone_id; the count of gap-followup sub-sprints auto-dispatched for that milestone. Fail-closed escalation to needs_human when a milestone reaches its max_subsprints bound.

gap_set_history.description: The remaining gap req_id-set (sorted) recorded at the START of each remediation round, for the §1.7-F clause 2 PROPER-SUBSET progress check (proper-subset, NOT identical-hash — catches A/B churn). The last entry is the prior round's set; a new round whose set is NOT a strict proper subset is a no-progress round.

no_progress_rounds.description: Consecutive no-progress (non-proper-subset) rounds; fail-closed escalation to needs_human when it reaches gap_followup.max_no_progress_rounds (§1.7-F clause 2/3).

gap_review_seq.description: Monotonic per-pause nonce counter: each completeness_gap_review pause stamps gap_review_seq into its checkpoint basename so the file-based decision resolver refuses a stale remediate file from an earlier round (Codex R1 B3 — the 'ONE bounded round' binding).

pending_remediation.description: Codex R2 B2 — the in-flight remediation marker, persisted BEFORE a gapfix dispatch and cleared in the atomic round-save. On a STATUS_RUNNING crash-recovery the engine RE-ENTERS this exact gapfix (resume=True) instead of re-dispatching it, so a Driver that completed before the crash returns its outcome without re-running (no double-run / no spend undercount). Present only transiently while a round is in flight.

scope_hash.description: Codex R4 B1 — the live F1 signed_scope_hash the round was authorized under. On crash-recovery _complete_pending_remediation refuses the marker if this no longer matches (a plan/charter edit between dispatch and resume), so a stale marker can never auto-run out-of-epoch/out-of-envelope work.

remediations.description: Audit log of every generated remediation sub-sprint stanza (§1.7-F clause 1), so the in-envelope covered_req_ids claim each round was dispatched on is reconstructable.

round.description: The 1-based per-milestone round this stanza was generated for.

milestone_outcomes.description: Δ-19 F3 (design §3.5.1). The runner stamps each milestone's TERMINAL close outcome here so scope_report can derive delivery_status deterministically (delivered vs waived-with-reason) — 'delivered' is NOT inferable from cursor position alone (a campaign can advance past a milestone that shipped without an Acceptance pass). Additive; absent ⇒ today's behavior.

milestone_id.description: The milestone whose terminal outcome this records.

terminal.description: The terminal close path (design §3.5.1 table). delivered ⟸ acceptance_pass_*; waived ⟸ fix_required_ship/surface_approve_ship/acceptance_off/out_of_scope_advance; not_shipped ⟸ rejected/aborted.

pause_reason.description: The last unit's pause_reason that produced this terminal outcome (audit linkage).

decision_ref.description: Pointer to the recorded human decision (checkpoint/decision file) when the close required one.

spent.description: Cumulative COUNTABLE spend (design §5.4a); checked between units against campaign.budget.

units.description: One record per dispatched sub-sprint unit, in dispatch order — the campaign audit linkage to each Driver loop_id.

final_state.description: The Driver final_state the run returned (advance|done|halted|gate1_pending|...).

loop_id.description: The Driver loop_id, for cross-referencing the per-sub-sprint audit ledger.

pause_reason.description: When this unit HALTED: the pause checkpoint_id, persisted INTO the record so a §3.5c STATUS_RUNNING crash-recovery REPLAY re-pauses from the recorded unit WITHOUT re-running it (no duplicate execution/Acceptance).

checkpoint_path.description: When this unit HALTED: the checkpoint file path, persisted alongside pause_reason for the same §3.5c crash-recovery replay (the human resolves it on resume).

attempt_nonce.description: Phase-4 (design §5.3). The per-(milestone,subsprint) monotonic dispatch counter this unit was folded under, so the exactly-once fold key is [loop_id, attempt_nonce] (a legit re-dispatch reuses loop_id but bumps the nonce; a crash re-delivery of the same nonce is a no-op). Absent on serial units (byte-identical); present only when the parallel coordinator folds a worker result.

halt_condition_acks.description: Phase-3 (design §3.4). PERMANENT halt-condition ack keys — a committed pre-dispatch halt cascade. Each key is a tuple [condition_id, condition_digest, milestone_id(, subsprint_id)] (gate-once-per-scope). Additive; absent/empty ⇒ no halt condition has ever been acknowledged (byte-identical to a pre-Phase-3 campaign).

halt_condition_provisional.description: Phase-3 (design §3.4). PROVISIONAL ack keys for the CURRENT pre-dispatch halt cascade — proceeded but not yet committed. Promoted en masse into halt_condition_acks at the redispatched unit's outcome save; flushed en masse when the plan is re-signed to a new epoch since the cascade's first halt. Additive; absent/empty ⇒ no cascade outstanding.

halt_condition_pending.description: Phase-3 (design §3.5). The DURABLE identity of the OUTSTANDING halt_condition_met pause (the file-based decision resolver binds to it). signed_scope_hash is the cascade epoch, captured at HALT time. Additive; absent/null ⇒ no halt_condition_met pause outstanding. When present it MUST be complete (the resolver identity-binds against it) — an incomplete record is a fail-closed state error (R2 NB-2).

halt_condition_seq.description: Phase-3 (design §3.5). Monotonic per-pause halt_condition_met NONCE counter (the checkpoint basename suffix). Additive; absent/0 ⇒ no halt_condition_met pause has been emitted.

milestone_runtime.description: Phase-4 (design §3.1). The PER-MILESTONE runtime map, keyed by milestone_id — the parallel runner's generalization of the singleton cursor/pause/overlay fields. Emitted ONLY when the parallel path is active (budget.max_concurrent>1); a serial campaign never populates it ⇒ byte-identical state.json. The top-level singleton fields mirror the OLDEST outstanding milestone pause for one deprecation cycle; the top-level cursor is the fail-closed (0,0) mirror (§3.2). engine_restamp, halt_condition_seq, halt_condition_acks, spent, units, gap_followup_state stay GLOBAL (coordinator-owned).

phase.description: ready=admissible; running=one sub-sprint in flight; paused=at a human gate; done=milestone accepted (branch NOT merged — a leaf may terminate here); merged=integrated into trunk (required for a dependency-target).

context.description: This milestone's git isolation context (milestone_context shape).

inflight.description: The ONE in-flight sub-sprint, written by a SINGLE pre-spawn _save() so everything the fold needs is durable BEFORE fork (§5.5). No pid/worker here — liveness is the parent-held-then-inherited flock + a worker-owned lease sidecar. A present (non-null) inflight MUST carry attempt_nonce + subsprint_id (the durable fold inputs); null ⇒ no sub-sprint in flight.

dispatch_freshness_slice.description: Canonical dispatch-time freshness snapshot: H wrapper + this milestone's _envelope_milestone entry + authority + the two *_digest fields (§5.6).

folded.description: Fold keys [loop_id, attempt_nonce] already folded (idempotent re-delivery guard, §5.3). Draft 2020-12 tuple validation uses prefixItems (NOT the legacy array-form items).

epoch_drift.description: Durable per-milestone drift gate {dispatch_freshness_slice, observed_freshness_slice}; clears ONLY on an exact freshness-slice equality re-check, never merely because the plan is signed (§5.6).
